name: Lagos and Salema Coastal Loop
name_original: De Lagos até Salema — percurso circular
description: This moderate 24.9-mile (40.0 km) road cycling route from Lagos to Salema offers coastal views and charming villages with 1537 feet (468 met
region_intro: No traffic road cycling routes around Lagos (Santa Maria) traverse a diverse landscape characterized by a dramatic coastline with sandstone cliffs and golden beaches, transitioning to rolling inland terrain. The region offers a mix of flat coastal stretches and more challenging hilly ascents, with well-maintained roads. Cyclists can explore minor, single-lane roads through farmland and small villages, as well as routes leading to higher elevations in the Serra de Monchique. Best no traffic road cycling routes around Lagos (Santa Maria) The most popular no traffic road cycling route is Lagos and Salema Coastal Loop, a 24.9 miles (40.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers extensive coastal views, connecting various beaches along the Algarve. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Porto de Mós Beach – Burgau Beach loop from Farol de Ponta da Piedade, a moderate 14.9 miles (24.0 km) path. This route provides scenic coastal riding with views of the Atlantic and access to several beaches. Local road cyclists also love the Ponta da Piedade Lighthouse – Lagos Marina loop from Farol de Ponta da Piedade, a 17.7 miles (28.5 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past the iconic Ponta da Piedade headland, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Road cycling around Lagos (Santa Maria) is defined by coastal paths, inland back roads, and hilly ascents towards the Serra de Monchique. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more demanding climbs. The routes in Lagos (Santa Maria) are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 160 reviews. More than 3,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Lagos (Santa Maria)'s varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic road cycling routes are available around Lagos (Santa Maria)?
A: There are over 25 dedicated no-traffic road cycling routes around Lagos (Santa Maria) listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy, flat rides to more challenging routes with significant climbs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on these no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: The no-traffic road cycling routes around Lagos (Santa Maria) offer a diverse terrain. You can expect a mix of rolling hills, coastal stretches, and inland back roads. Some routes feature gentle ascents and descents, while others, particularly those heading towards the Serra de Monchique, include more demanding climbs like the 11-kilometer ascent to Picota.

Q: Are there any easy no-traffic road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy no-traffic road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32237733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagos – Tree-Lined Road in Portugal loop from Lagos&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option with moderate elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed ride. The region generally offers quiet country lanes and dedicated cycle paths that are suitable for those looking for less challenging terrain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see along these routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by iconic landmarks. You can cycle near the dramatic golden sandstone cliffs and rock formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/130587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponta da Piedade&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the beautiful beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/122088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camilo Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/395482" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgau Beach&lt;/a&gt;. Inland, routes might take you through areas with views of the Serra de Monchique.

Q: Can I find circular no-traffic road cycling routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the no-traffic road cycling routes around Lagos (Santa Maria) are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422499433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagos and Salema Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 40km ride, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32219688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porto de Mós Beach – Burgau Beach loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for no-traffic road cycling in Lagos (Santa Maria)?
A: The Algarve's favorable climate allows for year-round cycling. However, the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months are particularly ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and less intense sun, making for comfortable rides on the region's quiet roads.

Q: Are there any no-traffic routes that offer coastal views?
A: Yes, the region is renowned for its coastal beauty. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422499433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagos and Salema Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt; provide stunning views of the Atlantic, connecting various beaches and offering glimpses of the dramatic coastline.

Q: What do other cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Lagos (Santa Maria)?
A: The komoot community highly rates the road cycling experience in Lagos (Santa Maria), with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the region's well-maintained roads, the stunning blend of coastal and inland landscapes, and the opportunity to find quiet routes away from heavy traffic.

Q: Are there options for longer no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate in length, you can find longer options or combine routes for extended rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422499433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagos and Salema Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a good example of a moderate 40km route, and the region's network of minor roads allows for longer explorations through farmland and small villages.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through or near traditional villages?
A: Many inland back roads offer the chance to cycle through charming small villages and farmland, providing a glimpse into the local culture away from the main tourist areas. These routes often feature gentle ascents and descents, connecting picturesque spots.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on these no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: Elevation gains vary significantly. Easier routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32237733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagos – Tree-Lined Road in Portugal loop&lt;/a&gt;, have around 230 meters of elevation gain. More challenging routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422499433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagos and Salema Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt;, can involve around 470 meters of climbing, especially when venturing into the rolling terrain north of the main highways.
tips: Bordered by the Atlantic Ocean, the city of Lagos, in Portugal, has natural beauty and a rich heritage. That's why it's very popular with tourists. Not to be missed: the Governors Castle, the Ponta da Bandeira fortress, the Infante D. Henrique square and the baroque church of Santo António.
A former small fishing village, Salema now welcomes tourists from all over the world. In its picturesque little streets, shops and services abound.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the From Lagos to Salema circular route?
faqs.answer: This road cycling route is approximately 40 km long and generally takes around 1 hour and 53 minutes to complete. This duration is for continuous cycling and doesn't include stops for sightseeing or breaks.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate. It features rolling terrain with a mix of ascents and descents, totaling about 470 meters of elevation gain. While most ascents are gradual, be aware of a short but steep 17% gradient hill after Boca do Rio. A good level of fitness is recommended.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally rolling and mostly well-paved with asphalt. However, some segments may include unpaved sections or cobblestones, which can add a challenge for road bikes. The route often uses smaller tarmac roads and backroads, offering a more immersive experience.

faqs.question: What scenic highlights will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy diverse and picturesque scenery. The route often hugs the coastline, providing panoramic views of the Atlantic Ocean, dramatic cliffs, and pristine beaches like Salema Beach. You'll also pass through charming fishing villages and the historic center of Lagos. Inland sections offer views of fields, orchards, and forested hills.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Lagos to Salema circular route?
faqs.answer: While the route is circular, many cyclists choose to start in Lagos. Look for public parking areas within or near the historic center of Lagos, or in Salema, depending on your preferred starting point. Specific parking details can vary, so checking local parking options upon arrival is advisable.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Algarve region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are often ideal, with comfortable temperatures and less crowded roads. Summer can be hot, especially midday, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if cycling during peak season.

faqs.question: Is the From Lagos to Salema circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route, it's generally not suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists, especially given the distance and potential for traffic. If you're looking for dog-friendly activities, consider local walking trails where dogs are permitted on a leash.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle the From Lagos to Salema circular route. It uses public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through Lagos and charming fishing villages like Salema and Burgau, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can stop for refreshments, a meal, or to refill your water bottles.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the EuroVelo 1 - Atlantic Coast Route - part Portugal 3, LGS BTT P5, and LGS BTT P7, among others. This offers opportunities to explore extended cycling networks if you wish.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, approximately 40 km distance, and 470 meters of elevation gain, this route is best suited for cyclists with a good level of fitness and some experience with road cycling. Beginners might find the distance and some of the climbs challenging.
